author: @Liam_Patton
text:
Why not a flap that changes angle when it hits the ends of the track?

Air pressure drives it to the right, it hits a tooth in the track that forces the aerofoil to flip over to the opposite direction, and the air pressure now pushes in the opposite direction.

Use the back and forth to run that button generator you just demonstrated.

author: @glencahalin4786
text:
I would have thought that there'd be a natural frequency of oscillation from the wind, which you'd want to tune, probably the softest possible, springs to for maximum displacement.

What the vortex shedding  visual made me think is, that vortex itself should produce oscillation on anything behind it, leading me to ponder if it'd be better to think of the whole system in 3D eg 3 windbars at the front, 2 ( maybe 3 ) slightly offset behind and maybe 3 more behind that. It wouldn't add much depth to the overall package but should produce more energy from the 2D front area limitation that people will have.

author: @justtinkering6713
text: Here's a thought. How about a steam engine? Use a monotube boiler for safety, drive a Tesla turbine connected to a generator. Condense the steam back into water for reuse. Lots more power than Stirling. It would be an awesome project.

replies.author: @ياقوت-ق
replies.text: Typically, a Tesla turbine is used when there are viscous fluids, so I think using blades will give greater output and it is simpler. There is a difference between a turbine and an engine. The turbine produces a high RPM but less torque, while the engine produces a lower RPM with high torque. Each has applications that suit it, and certainly adding a simple system such as gears will improve the performance, efficiency and reliability of both turbine and engine Significantly.

author: @richardglickman7203

author: @DarkWolfsDen
text:
Inside the vertical bars you will want to have solid bars that feed downwards into a flywheel similar to your project in 2046 on each side (2 flywheels per horizontal bar), then you only need springs that will prevent "bottoming out" on the bottom and springs at the top to prevent "crashing".

No need for the springs to actually attach to the "bar" directly, they just attach at the top/bottom of the vertical bars while the horizontal bar can be "free floating".

author: @stevewebb5735
text:
Ring magnets sliding on rods to act as magnetic springs?...or multiple ring magnets on same rod to increase "spring" length.
Non-linear spring constant - weak for small displacement and strong for wide travel - may help get oscillation going, also try tuning the mass
There might also be a way to extract power using the same magnets.
Friction could bea problem - small diameter rod
Do a search on kjmagnetics and magnetic pogo-stick - they 3d printed a test model

author: @ivanfreedom
text:
Firstly I though about a turbine but a windbeam will works, maybe better what do you think about?:   Vehicle-Induced Water Pressure Energy Harvesting System

Objective: To design and implement an energy harvesting system that utilizes the mechanical energy of passing vehicles to pressurize water, drive a turbine, generate electricity, and recirculate the water for continuous operation.

System Components:

    Water-Filled Bag:
        A robust, flexible bag filled with water, positioned on the road surface to be compressed by the passing vehicles.
        Materials selection should prioritize durability, flexibility, and resistance to wear and tear.

    Valve and Turbine Assembly:
        A valve mechanism to control the flow of pressurized water from the bag to the turbine.
        A turbine designed to convert the kinetic energy of the flowing water into rotational energy.

    Electric Generator:
        Coupled to the turbine to convert mechanical energy into electrical energy.

    Energy Storage Cylinder:
        A mechanism to temporarily hold the water post-turbine operation before it's recirculated back into the bag.

    Water Recirculation System:
        A system to ensure the water returns to the bag, maintaining a continuous cycle of water flow for sustained energy harvesting.

    Control System:
        A control system to manage the operation of the valve, monitor the performance of the turbine and generator, and oversee the recirculation of water.

Operational Flow:

    Vehicle Compression: As vehicles pass over the water-filled bag, the water is pressurized.
    Turbine Rotation: The pressurized water flows through the valve, driving the turbine to rotate and generate electricity.
    Water Collection: Post-turbine, water is collected in the energy storage cylinder.
    Water Recirculation: Water is recirculated back to the bag, ready for the next cycle of energy harvesting.

replies.author: @ivanfreedom
replies.text: @christopherd.winnan8701 I didn't knew how to explain my idea so chatGPT came to help me! (apes also use tools).  I thought in the water because its not compressible, directing most of the energy from the car weight directly to the turbine or windbend, and later a cylinder with some air, which is compressible and will act as energy storage, also responsible to return the fluid(water) to the start of the cycle. Indeed air is a good option to reduce drastically the complexity. I appreciate your comments, so thanks!

replies.author: @ivanfreedom
replies.text: @christopherd.winnan8701  This is the GPT prompt I used: "Please describe the idea in a way I will explain to the Robert Muray Smith, an engineer, to do it"

author: @gerryjamesedwards1227
text: Hi Rob, have you seen those old spring reverb units that Fender used to make to go with guitar amps? They used two simple  transducers to both to drive one end of the spring and to recover the signal from the other end  of the spring, in each case using a twisting motion from the lorenz force. Could these sorts of transducers be beefed up and used to generate from a wind-belt or bar?
